content:
And so the IBM AI Racing League has come to an end for our team “WeShowSpeed”— and it has been a truly exciting and valuable experience for us.
At first, we saw this challenge mainly as a racing task: improve the car, make it faster, avoid crashes, and achieve better lap times. But very quickly it became much more than that. We started experimenting with different approaches, analyzing the car’s behavior, testing many versions of the controller, and learning how small changes in speed, braking, steering, and acceleration can completely change the result.
One of the most interesting parts of this journey was working with different LLMs. We spent a lot of time discussing ideas, generating possible solutions, checking code, analyzing logs, and trying to understand how AI tools can help us improve the car’s performance. This process helped us dive deeper into the world of Artificial Intelligence and understand how important testing, data analysis, and patience are in real AI-related projects.
There were many difficult moments: the car was sometimes too slow, sometimes too aggressive, sometimes unstable in corners, and sometimes unpredictable on different parts of the track. But every mistake gave us new experience. Every failed attempt helped us understand the system better and improve our thinking.
The IBM AI Racing League showed us that AI is not only about theory. It is also about experimenting, solving problems, testing ideas, learning from results, and constantly improving. It was a great opportunity to connect programming, simulation, racing logic, and Artificial Intelligence in one practical project.
We are very grateful to IBM for giving us this opportunity. This competition motivated us to learn more, work harder, and explore AI from a practical point of view. It was an interesting, challenging, and inspiring experience that we will definitely remember.
Thank you, IBM, for this amazing journey! 🏁🤖
